methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Strata Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $5.2M to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 private peers in the robotics sector (including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics) and 1 public comparable.

The stage-progression method is applicable and provides the primary valuation signal. Using total raised ($5.2M) as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields $13M. A peer-median approach is not reliable given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics sector; while 12 same-sector companies exist, fewer than 3 meet narrow stage and valuation-tier filters, making broader sector medians unrepresentative.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a valuation range of $11M to $15M, with a median of $13M. Key uncertainties include the strength and relevance of the robotics peer set at comparable stages, the appropriate step-up multiplier given market conditions, and product-market traction not captured in capitalization history. Further diligence on revenue, unit economics, and technology differentiation would be necessary to refine the estimate within this range.
